html:
GenericEmptySome honeyFullBurnt

“If there are 6 or more flowers around a bee box, bees will produce honey inside the bee box.”

-

A Bee Box is a craftable Structure that uses Bees to accumulate Honey that can be harvested. Bee Boxes are found in the Food Tab and require a Science Machine to prototype. It takes 1 Honeycomb, 4 Bees, and 2 Boards to build. When destroyed by a Hammer, it will return 1 Honeycomb and 1 Boards, and will release any Bees inside the Box.

Bee Boxes are also prime targets for Bearger attacks.

Beehaviour

A Bee Box will (re)spawn a new Bee every 120 seconds up to the maximum population of 4 per box. All Bees will return to their box at dusk, but they will leave it during daytime one at a time every 15–30 seconds, depending on the length of daytime. During Winter, they won't leave the Box.

Technically, Bees do not have a fixed role. Instead, the same specimen can act as a worker or a fighter depending on what the current situation calls for. Every time a Bee leaves the hive, the game assigns it a role. When a Bee returns to the hive, that role gets erased. Therefore, the same specimen can fight off a threat as a Killer Bee, enter the hive, and then re-emerge as a worker to gather pollen.

If a Bee is attacked or caught with a Bug Net, all members of the hive still inside the box will emerge immediately as Killer Bees and engage. Additionally, Bees of the same hive, and homeless bees 30 Tiles of the attacked or caught bee - killer or otherwise - will join the fight. The combined number of attackers coming to help cannot exceed 10 unless the player attacks again.

When a bee box is harvested, all remaining Bees in the Bee Box will emerge as workers and attack while those that were already outside will ignore the player unless the player fights back. If it is winter, the bees will not emerge to attack when honey is harvested (but they also don't emerge to produce honey during this time).

Honey Production

The speed at which Bees gather Honey is affected by the quantity and proximity to Flowers near a Bee Box. If a player is within 20 tiles, honey production follows these rules:

  • Every time a Bee successfully pollinates 6 Flowers, it will return home and increase the Honey contained within by 1. The same Flower cannot be pollinated twice by the same Bee within one trip or by two Bees at the same time.
  • If a Bee doesn't manage to pollinate enough Flowers before dusk, it will have to start over the following morning.
  • Having less than 6 Flowers around a Bee Box halts production entirely. Ideally, 6 butterflies are caught and planted beside the bee box, or where the bees naturally like to land nearby.

If no player is within 20 tiles of the bee box, Honey will be produced at a rate of 1 per day if at least one flower is nearby.

Bee Boxes can contain anywhere between 0 and 6 Honey. Visually they progress through 4 different stages to indicate Honey saturation at 0, 1-2, 3-5 and 6 units respectively. For example, a bee box at stage 3 (almost full) may contain between 3 and 5 pieces of Honey.

The four harvest phases of the Bee Box.

A quick player can harvest from a bee box, run away before getting stung, and the bees will give up the chase after a screen or so. Bees will stay in their box and not attack during Winter. One could light the bee box briefly on fire to evacuate the bees before harvesting the honey, but care must be taken to extinguish the fire after the bees have left but before destroying the bee box. An Ice Flingomatic can put out the fire automatically to save time in a large honey farm.

Bees can produce honey in the Caves, much like on the surface, but there are no naturally spawning flowers or beehives there. Flowers planted under Cave Lights will grow normally, but any planted under insufficient natural light will wilt in a day and can never be revived. Bees will exit their box during the day when there is sufficient light to ward of Charlie (natural or artificial), but since only flowers near the box count toward pollination, it's best to locate the whole honey farm under a large Cave Light.

There is an ideal distance from base to place a bee box so that honey is produced using the quicker method, but aggressive spring bees are unlikely to attack the player or any followers (angry bees can swarm and kill pigs, merms, an unmounted beefalo, etc.).

Hermit Bee Box

The Hermit Bee Box is a naturally spawning object exclusive to Don't Starve Together, introduced in Return of Them. One can be found on the Hermit Island.

The Hermit Bee Box works just like a normal bee box, exceptions being that it cannot be destroyed or burned.

Tips

Adequate protection against hostile bees.
  • Bees are slow and can easily be outrun if aggravated. Their kite attacks can even create a larger distance between themselves and the player. It will only take a few seconds of running before Bees will lose interest.
    • Equipping a Walking Cane or building a Cobblestone path to the Bee Box can prove useful. A player can use the increased movement speed to harvest Honey without fear of retaliation if timed correctly.

Trivia

  • In the old science system, it used to cost 100 Research Points to craft.
  • The honeycomb slot on top of Bee Boxes will appear to fill with Honey as more Honey is stored within.
  • The Hermit Bee Box was introduced to Don't Starve Together in the She Sells Sea Shells update.

Bugs

  • Occasionally at dusk, Bees will get stuck next to their Box. New Bees do not spawn when there are stuck Bees. They will stay this way indefinitely until caught or attacked. Reloading the game will work too.
  • Sometimes, Bees from the Bee Box will waste time by spinning constantly over Flowers until dusk. This can be fixed by attacking the bee or picking the Flowers that are causing the problem, or by reloading the game.

Skins

Bee Box Skins

Main article: Belongings

  • The Woven - Elegant Home Sweet Home skin was introduced during the official Year of the Carrat event in 2020.
  • The Woven - Elegant Bee Basket skin was introduced during the official Year of the Beefalo event in 2021.
  • The Loyal Crystalline Honeydome skin was awarded for watching 6 hours of Don't Starve Together on Twitch from April 23, 2020 to May 28, 2020, and from February 11, 2021 to February 18, 2021. It is also available in the Klei Rewards shop for 1,200 Klei points..
  • The Loyal Butterbee skin was awarded for watching 3 hours of Don't Starve Together on Twitch from April 16, 2026 to May 14, 2026.

"beebox"
GenericEmptySome honeyFullBurnt
“Bees!”
–Wilson
“Come on fat bees, make honey!”
–Willow
“Many bees!”
–Wolfgang
“Their stolen labor tastes so sweet.”
–Wendy
“WORK HARDER, BEES”
–WX-78
“A crude apiary.”
–Wickerbottom
“Buzzers!”
–Woodie
“It holds the bees.”
–Maxwell
“A sweet böx öf wee warriörs.”
–Wigfrid
“It's a box with bees in it.”
–Webber
“Home of the honeymakers!”
–Warly
“Buzz home!”
–Wormwood
“It's like an assembly line in there. Busy!”
–Winona
“A box full of bees would make a great prank.”
–Wortox
“Home for stingers.”
–Wurt
“Oh bees, why can't we be friends...”
–Walter
“I thought I heard some buzzing coming from over here.”
–Wanda
“It's empty.”
–Wilson
“Nothing to see here.”
–Willow
“It has no honey.”
–Wolfgang
“Work, bees!”
–Wendy
“NO HONEY DETECTED”
–WX-78
“It's devoid of honey.”
–Wickerbottom
“I'm getting hungry just looking at it.”
–Woodie
“Why do the bees not serve me?”
–Maxwell
“Where's the höney?”
–Wigfrid
“There's no honey inside.”
–Webber
“No more honey...”
–Warly
“Empty”
–Wormwood
“Where's that stellar work ethic, bees?!”
–Winona
“Nothing inside but bees, oh yes!”
–Wortox
“Aww no sweets inside.”
–Wurt
“They need a bit more time.”
–Walter
“Not even a drop of honey inside.”
–Wanda
“Need to wait a bit.”
–Wilson
“Patience.”
–Willow
“Bees are busy.”
–Wolfgang
“Toil on.”
–Wendy
“HONEY LEVELS ARE LOW”
–WX-78
“It's not ready for harvesting.”
–Wickerbottom
“I could get more if I waited.”
–Woodie
“I should wait a bit longer.”
–Maxwell
“Pithy höney. Möre patience is needed.”
–Wigfrid
“Work faster, bees!”
–Webber
“There is a little honey.”
–Warly
“Wait”
–Wormwood
“You've been working hard.”
–Winona
“We could scrape a bit of honey out.”
–Wortox
“Don't wanna wait!!”
–Wurt
“They're still busy making honey.”
–Walter
“Why does honey take so long to make?”
–Wanda
“It's full of honey.”
–Wilson
“Yay! Let's steal honey!”
–Willow
“Is ready for harvest!”
–Wolfgang
“Their toils have been fruitful!”
–Wendy
“HONEY LEVELS ARE HIGH”
–WX-78
“Honey can be harvested from it.”
–Wickerbottom
“Maple syrup substitute!”
–Woodie
“So tempting, but so full of bees.”
–Maxwell
“It's a höney treasure tröve!”
–Wigfrid
“It's full to brimming.”
–Webber
“Honey jackpot!”
–Warly
“Buzz Juice ready!”
–Wormwood
“Excellent work, bees!”
–Winona
“So much honey! The mortals will be overjoyed!”
–Wortox
“Sweets!!”
–Wurt
“Look at all that honey!”
–Walter
“Finally!”
–Wanda
“How did it get burned?!!”
–Wilson
“Smoked you out!”
–Willow
“Bees are burned.”
–Wolfgang
“Burnt honey.”
–Wendy
“ACTIVITY LEVELS AT ZERO”
–WX-78
“Poor bees!”
–Wickerbottom
“No, not the bees!”
–Woodie
“Honey roasted.”
–Maxwell
“The hive is silent.”
–Wigfrid
“The site of The Great Honey Fire.”
–Webber
“Disastrously caramelized.”
–Warly
“Fire breaks everything”
–Wormwood
“Factory fire!”
–Winona
“Mayhaps it's caramelized within!”
–Wortox
“It not buzzing anymore...”
–Wurt
“What! Who burned the hive?”
–Walter
“It looks like its honey making days are behind it.”
–Wanda
“If there are 6 or more flowers around a bee box, bees will produce honey inside the bee box.”

The Hermit Bee Box is a naturally spawning object exclusive to Don't Starve Together, introduced in Return of Them. One can be found on the Hermit Island.
The Hermit Bee Box works just like a normal bee box, exceptions being that it cannot be destroyed or burned.
Tips[edit | edit source]
Adequate protection against hostile bees.
Bees are slow and can easily be outrun if aggravated. Their kite attacks can even create a larger distance between themselves and the player. It will only take a few seconds of running before Bees will lose interest.
Equipping a Walking Cane or building a Cobblestone path to the Bee Box can prove useful. A player can use the increased movement speed to harvest Honey without fear of retaliation if timed correctly.
Trivia[edit | edit source]
In the old science system, it used to cost 100 Research Points to craft.
The honeycomb slot on top of Bee Boxes will appear to fill with Honey as more Honey is stored within.
The Hermit Bee Box was introduced to Don't Starve Together in the She Sells Sea Shells update.
Bugs[edit | edit source]
Occasionally at dusk, Bees will get stuck next to their Box. New Bees do not spawn when there are stuck Bees. They will stay this way indefinitely until caught or attacked. Reloading the game will work too.
Sometimes, Bees from the Bee Box will waste time by spinning constantly over Flowers until dusk. This can be fixed by attacking the bee or picking the Flowers that are causing the problem, or by reloading the game.
Skins[edit | edit source]
Bee Box Skins[edit | edit source]
Main article: Belongings
The Woven - Elegant Home Sweet Home skin was introduced during the official Year of the Carrat event in 2020.
The Woven - Elegant Bee Basket skin was introduced during the official Year of the Beefalo event in 2021.
The Loyal Crystalline Honeydome skin was awarded for watching 6 hours of Don't Starve Together on Twitch from April 23, 2020 to May 28, 2020, and from February 11, 2021 to February 18, 2021. It is also available in the Klei Rewards shop for 1,200 Klei points..
The Loyal Butterbee skin was awarded for watching 3 hours of Don't Starve Together on Twitch from April 16, 2026 to May 14, 2026.

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Hộp Ong là một Công trình có thể chế tạo được, sử dụng Ong để tích lũy Mật ong có thể thu hoạch được. Hộp Ong được tìm thấy trong Thẻ Thức Ăn và yêu cầu Máy Khoa Học để tạo nguyên mẫu. Cần 1 Tổ Ong, 4 Ong và 2 Ván Ván để xây dựng. Khi bị Búa phá hủy, nó sẽ trả lại 1 Tổ ong và 1 Ván ép, đồng thời sẽ thả bất kỳ Ong nào ở trong Hộp.

The mortals will be overjoyed! |wormwood = Buzz Juice ready! |wurt = Sweets!! |walter = Look at all that honey! |wanda = Finally! |choose = wilson }} |-|Burnt= {{Quotes |wilson = How did it get burned?!! |willow = Smoked you out! |wolfgang = Bees are burned. |wendy = Burnt honey. |wx78 = ACTIVITY LEVELS AT ZERO |wickerbottom = Poor bees! |woodie = No, not the bees! |maxwell = Honey roasted. |wigfrid = The hive is silent. |webber = The site of The Great Honey Fire. |winona = Factory fire! |warly = Disastrously caramelized. |wortox = Mayhaps it's caramelized within! |wormwood = Fire breaks everything |wurt = It not buzzing anymore... |walter = What! Who burned the hive? |wanda = It looks like its honey making days are behind it. |choose = wilson }} {{Scrapbook_Info|If there are 6 or more flowers around a bee box, bees will produce honey inside the bee box.}} A '''Bee Box''' is a [[craftable]] [[Structure]] that uses [[Bee]]s to accumulate [[Honey]] that can be harvested. Bee Boxes are found in the [[Food Tab]] and require a [[Science Machine]] to prototype. It takes 1 [[Honeycomb]], 4 Bees, and 2 [[Boards]] to build. When destroyed by a [[Hammer]], it will return 1 Honeycomb and 1 Boards, and will release any Bees inside the Box. Bee Boxes are also prime targets for [[Bearger]] attacks. == {{Pic32|Bee}} Beehaviour == A Bee Box will (re)spawn a new Bee every 120 seconds up to the maximum population of 4 per box. All Bees will return to their box at dusk, but they will leave it during daytime one at a time every 15–30 seconds, depending on the length of daytime. During [[Winter]], they won't leave the Box. Technically, Bees do not have a fixed role. Instead, the same specimen can act as a worker or a fighter depending on what the current situation calls for. Every time a Bee leaves the hive, the game assigns it a role. When a Bee returns to the hive, that role gets erased. Therefore, the same specimen can fight off a threat as a [[Killer Bee]], enter the hive, and then re-emerge as a worker to gather pollen. If a Bee is attacked or caught with a [[Bug Net]], all members of the hive still inside the box will emerge immediately as Killer Bees and engage. Additionally, Bees of the same hive, and homeless bees 30 [[Tile]]s of the attacked or caught bee - killer or otherwise - will join the fight. The combined number of attackers coming to help cannot exceed 10 unless the player attacks again. When a bee box is harvested, all remaining Bees in the Bee Box will emerge as workers and attack while those that were already outside will ignore the player unless the player fights back. If it is winter, the bees will not emerge to attack when honey is harvested (but they also don't emerge to produce honey during this time). == {{Pic32|honey}} Honey Production == The speed at which Bees gather Honey is affected by the quantity and proximity to [[Flower]]s near a Bee Box. If a player is within 20 tiles, honey production follows these rules: * Every time a Bee successfully pollinates 6 Flowers, it will return home and increase the Honey contained within by 1. The same Flower cannot be pollinated twice by the same Bee within one trip or by two Bees at the same time. * If a Bee doesn't manage to pollinate enough Flowers before dusk, it will have to start over the following morning. * Having less than 6 Flowers around a Bee Box halts production entirely. Ideally, 6 butterflies are caught and planted beside the bee box, or where the bees naturally like to land nearby. If no player is within 20 tiles of the bee box, Honey will be produced at a rate of 1 per day if at least one flower is nearby. Bee Boxes can contain anywhere between 0 and 6 Honey. Visually they progress through 4 different stages to indicate Honey saturation at 0, 1-2, 3-5 and 6 units respectively. For example, a bee box at stage 3 (almost full) may contain between 3 and 5 pieces of Honey. [[File:Bee Box phases.png|thumb|600px|center|The four harvest phases of the Bee Box.|alt=]] A quick player can harvest from a bee box, run away before getting stung, and the bees will give up the chase after a screen or so. Bees will stay in their box and not attack during Winter. One could light the bee box briefly on fire to evacuate the bees before harvesting the honey, but care must be taken to extinguish the fire after the bees have left but before destroying the bee box. An [[Ice Flingomatic]] can put out the fire automatically to save time in a large honey farm. Bees can produce honey in the [[Caves]], much like on the surface, but there are no naturally spawning flowers or beehives there. Flowers planted under [[Cave Light]]s will grow normally, but any planted under insufficient ''natural'' light will wilt in a day and can never be revived. Bees will exit their box during the day when there is sufficient light to ward of Charlie (natural or artificial), but since only flowers near the box count toward pollination, it's best to locate the whole honey farm under a large Cave Light. There is an ideal distance from base to place a bee box so that honey is produced using the quicker method, but aggressive spring bees are unlikely to attack the player or any followers (angry bees can swarm and kill pigs, merms, an unmounted beefalo, etc.). == Hermit Bee Box == {{Object Infobox |picked = {{Pic32|Honey}} |image = Hermit Bee Box.png |spawnCode = "beebox_hermit" |spawns = {{pic|30|Bee}} {{pic|30|Killer Bee}} }} {{Quotes |wilson = It's full of honey. |willow = She won't be mad if I steal a liiiittle honey, right? |wolfgang = Is ready for harvest! |wendy = Their toils have been fruitful! |wx78 = HONEY LEVELS ARE HIGH |wickerbottom = Honey can be harvested from it. |woodie = Maple syrup substitute! |maxwell = I've done enough, the old woman can brave the bees herself. |wigfrid = It's a höney treasure tröve! |webber = Hey Ms. old lady! Look at all the honey they made! |winona = Excellent work, bees! |warly = Madame, I believe your honey is ready! |wortox = So much honey! That crabby mortal might crack a smile! |wormwood = Buzz Juice ready! |wurt = It look full of sweet stuff now! |walter = Don't panic Walter, the bees smell your fear... |wanda = She's quite creative with her limited materials. |choose = wortox }} The '''Hermit''' '''Bee Box''' is a naturally spawning object exclusive to ''[[Don't Starve Together]]'', introduced in ''[[Return of Them]]''. One can be found on the [[Hermit Island]]. The Hermit Bee Box works just like a normal bee box, exceptions being that it cannot be destroyed or burned. == {{Pic32|Prototype}} Tips == [[File:BeeKeeper Hat.jpg|thumb|120px|Adequate protection against hostile bees.]] * Bees are slow and can easily be outrun if aggravated. Their kite attacks can even create a larger distance between themselves and the player. It will only take a few seconds of running before Bees will lose interest. ** Equipping a [[Walking Cane]] or building a [[Cobblestones|Cobblestone]] path to the Bee Box can prove useful. A player can use the increased movement speed to harvest Honey without fear of retaliation if timed correctly. == {{pic32|Placeholder}} Trivia == *In the old science system, it used to cost 100 [[Research points list|Research Points]] to craft. *The honeycomb slot on top of Bee Boxes will appear to fill with Honey as more Honey is stored within. * The Hermit Bee Box was introduced to ''Don't Starve Together'' in the ''[[Return of Them#She Sells Sea Shells|She Sells Sea Shells]]'' update. == {{pic32|Mosquito}} Bugs == * Occasionally at dusk, Bees will get stuck next to their Box. New Bees do not spawn when there are stuck Bees. They will stay this way indefinitely until caught or attacked. Reloading the game will work too. * Sometimes, Bees from the Bee Box will waste time by spinning constantly over Flowers until dusk. This can be fixed by attacking the bee or picking the Flowers that are causing the problem, or by reloading the game. == [[File:Skins Icon.png|32px|link=]] Skins == ===Bee Box Skins=== {{Main|Belongings#Gardening and Food Skins|Belongings}} * The '''Woven - Elegant Home Sweet Home''' skin was introduced during the official [[Year of the Carrat]] event in 2020. * The '''Woven - Elegant Bee Basket''' skin was introduced during the official [[Year of the Beefalo]] event in 2021. * The '''Loyal Crystalline Honeydome''' skin was awarded for watching 6 hours of ''Don't Starve Together'' on Twitch from April 23, 2020 to May 28, 2020, and from February 11, 2021 to February 18, 2021. It is also available in the Klei Rewards shop for 1,200 Klei points.. * The '''Loyal Butterbee''' skin was awarded for watching 3 hours of ''Don't Starve Together'' on Twitch from April 16, 2026 to May 14, 2026.
{{SkinInfo |image = Bee Basket Icon.png |rarity = Woven - Elegant |collection = Victorian Collection |name = Bee Basket |description = The bees are all abuzz over this antique hive. }} {{SkinInfo |image = Home Sweet Home Icon.png |rarity = Woven - Elegant |collection = Year of the Carrat Collection |name = Home Sweet Home |description = This charming little abode is sure to put your bees at ease. |ingame = Home Sweet Home.png }} {{SkinInfo |image = Vintage Apiary Icon.png |rarity = Woven - Elegant |collection = Cottage Collection |name = Vintage Apiary |description = The rustic design is all the bzzzzzz. }} {{SkinInfo |image = Crystalline Honeydome Icon.png |rarity = Loyal |collection = Crystalline Collection |name = Crystalline Honeydome |description = A dazzling amber dome where bees make liquid gold. }} {{SkinInfo |image = Butterbee Icon.png |rarity = Loyal |collection = Chitinous Collection |name = Butterbee |description = Mixing honey and butter, who would have thought? }}
